key_features: Dual-user identity model (service providers and recipients), blockchain-backed data storage, credit scoring system, cross-platform support (iOS/Android/Web)
relevance_to_viecz: Direct competitor in campus errand service space. Demonstrates dual-user identity model for both requesting and providing services. Uses blockchain for data backup and credit system. Validates market demand for campus-specific errand platforms. Published in IEEE 2022, showing academic interest in this space.

key_features: Zero-commission model, mandatory .edu email verification, hyperlocal search (prioritizes university-specific results), real-time in-app chat, user reputation system with ratings and reviews, peer-to-peer buying/selling/trading
relevance_to_viecz: Recent 2025 academic publication demonstrating validated market gap in campus peer-to-peer services. Addresses 'Hyperlocal Trust Deficit' with closed-ecosystem approach. Zero-commission model solves market thickness problem. Technology stack (React.js, Node.js, MongoDB) provides architectural reference. Exemplifies trust-based hyperlocal verification.

key_features: College student marketplace for posting and fulfilling errands, marketplace for busy college students
relevance_to_viecz: Early student-focused errand marketplace demonstrating proof-of-concept for campus gig economy. Educational project (67-443 Mobile App Development course). Shows student demand for simplified errand sharing within campus communities.

key_features: Service marketplace connecting taskers and requesters, available on App Store, task/job/errand fulfillment platform
relevance_to_viecz: Active Southeast Asian task marketplace. Directly relevant to regional expansion. Connects skilled taskers with requesters in community settings, similar model to Viecz.

target_user: Students, professionals, gig workers, homemakers, retirees
key_features: On-demand gig work platform, integrated EPF savings system (unique to Malaysia), fast payment (3x/week), available jobs: warehouse crew, retail, F&B, delivery driving
user_count: [uncertain] Operating in Klang Valley, Penang, Negeri Sembilan, Johor Bahru
relevance_to_viecz: Active Southeast Asian gig platform with significant student focus. Awarded Best Recruitment Portal 2024 and Best Gig Workforce Management Platforms 2024 (Runner Up). Integrated financial services (EPF) shows regulatory sophistication. Demonstrates viability of gig model in Malaysian market.

key_features: On-demand moving and furniture delivery, specialized college/dorm moving, bookable within 30 minutes, 1M+ moves completed, 7500+ zip code coverage
relevance_to_viecz: Successful focused marketplace for student-adjacent services (moving). Demonstrates demand for hyperlocal logistics among student demographic. High-touch service model. Not campus-specific errands but adjacent service category.

key_features: Student-to-vendor marketplace, escrow payment system, seamless digital transactions, vendor/student matching, eliminates information gaps
relevance_to_viecz: Active campus marketplace in African market (emerging economy context similar to Southeast Asia). Demonstrates demand for campus-specific commerce platforms. Escrow system ensures transaction safety. Ecosystem approach (students + vendors).

country: Southeast Asia (Singapore, Indonesia, Thailand, Philippines, Malaysia, Vietnam)
target_user: General public, students via discount programs
key_features: Multi-service super-app including ride-hailing, food delivery, express parcel delivery, student discount plans (20-30% off in various countries), same-day delivery available
user_count: [uncertain] Operates across SE Asia with millions of users
relevance_to_viecz: Dominant regional player. GrabExpress offers task/delivery services. Student discount programs show market awareness. Regional presence in all major SE Asian markets. Not campus-focused but infrastructure competitor.

key_features: Super-app with 18+ services including ride-hailing, delivery, logistics, healthcare, home services
current_status: [uncertain] Reported to be merged/acquired by Grab in 2025
user_count: [uncertain] Millions across Indonesia and SE Asia
relevance_to_viecz: Regional competitor. Wide service coverage. Recent merger with Grab signals market consolidation. Demonstrates sustainable business model in SE Asia. Not campus-specific.

key_features: Home services marketplace (cleaning, pest control, plumbing, handymen), maintenance services, operated in 6 cities
relevance_to_viecz: Failed platform in South Asia. Note: Not student-focused. Demonstrates market dynamics and reasons for platform failure in India. Service-based marketplace that didn't sustain.

target_user: Students and gig workers in Philippines
key_features: Remote task/gig marketplace, low barrier to entry, student-friendly rates, virtual assistant tasks, content creation, freelance work
user_count: [uncertain] Millions of users globally, significant Philippines presence
relevance_to_viecz: Global remote work platforms heavily used by Filipino students. Not hyperlocal/campus-specific. Demonstrates student participation in gig economy. Alternative to in-person services.
